text: Machine learning is a way for computers to learn from data without being explicitly programmed. One common type is supervised learning, where the computer uses training data—a set of examples that include both inputs and correct outputs. The computer builds a model based on this data, which is like a set of rules or patterns it discovers. Once the model is ready, it can make predictions on new, unseen data. For example, after seeing many pictures of cats and dogs with labels, the model can predict whether a new photo shows a cat or a dog. The accuracy of these predictions is then measured to see how well the model has learned.
